headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the most negative comments on limited evidence, mainly over copyright and aggressive business concerns [E1][E3], while Anthropic landed mixed-neutral because one cautionary remark was offset by a positive note about Claude Code learning mode [E1][E2].

ranking.summary: OpenAI’s tone was negative on limited evidence, with criticism about vacuuming up content and weakening copyright claims plus broader worries about future price hikes and competitor buyouts [E1][E3].
Anthropic’s tone was mixed-neutral, balancing a warning about potential price hikes and acquisitions against praise for Claude Code’s Learning mode as a helpful learning aid [E1][E2].

evidence.excerpt: If OpenAI and Anthropic eventually become public companies with trillion-dollar valuations, it will be interesting to see if their company ethos remains the same. With that much purchasing power, it's very tempting to gobble up competitors and raise prices.

annotations.rationale: The commenter expresses concern that OpenAI, if it becomes a giant public company, would be tempted to acquire rivals and increase prices.
The commenter makes the same cautionary claim about Anthropic potentially using scale and public-market pressure to buy competitors and raise prices.

evidence.excerpt: For those using Claude Code, I recommend Learning mode to instruct Claude to walk you through implementing the solution yourself rather than doing it for you. It’s very helpful when diving into a new domain, and helps build lower level intuition.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly recommends Claude Code's Learning mode and says it is very helpful for learning and building intuition.

evidence.excerpt: On the other hand Microsoft is very much leading with OpenAI in vacuuming any content, stripping effectively copyright claims.

annotations.rationale: The commenter sharply criticizes OpenAI as participating in indiscriminate content collection and undermining copyright protections.
